message.content: Adversaries may compromise third-party web services like GitHub, Twitter, Dropbox, and Google to support cyber operations. By taking over legitimate user accounts, they can use these services for Command and Control (C2) or data exfiltration, blending into normal traffic and making detection challenging. This tactic allows adversaries to obscure their activities and avoid attribution. Notable groups like Earth Lusca and Turla have exploited Google Drive and WordPress sites, respectively, for such purposes. Mitigation strategies include enforcing strong, unique passwords, enabling multi-factor authentication, monitoring for unusual access patterns, and educating users on phishing and account security. Regular audits and the use of security tools to detect anomalies in web service usage can also help in early detection and response.
